narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Climate cultists push bizarre scare language about weather: "rain bombs" and "boiling oceans"
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text criticizes the climate movement by using the term 'climate cultists' and implying that their language is 'bizarre' and scare-mongering.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Criticism of institutions and authorities
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Gore would know about fossil fuel use, seeing as how he uses lots and lots of it – much more than the average person, in fact – to power his lavish, sprawling estates and fuel his private jets.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text criticizes Al Gore, a prominent figure in the climate movement and a former vice president, by highlighting his perceived hypocrisy in using fossil fuels despite advocating against them.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: They have been losing their minds for decades. I have been hearing about these end of the world scenarios since I was a kid. And not one of their predictions has come true.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text downplays the significance of climate change by implying that predictions of catastrophic consequences have been consistently incorrect.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Questioning the measurements and science
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: A few years ago, someone (John Nolte?) listed all of their bigger predictions and their track record was 0-47
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text questions the accuracy of climate science by citing a claimed track record of failed predictions, implying that the science is unreliable or flawed.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: The legislation contains $369 billion, not including tax credits, in appropriation funds for "renewable energy," meaning windmills, solar panels, and a very dark and cold future for humanity.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text expresses skepticism about green technologies, implying that they will lead to a 'dark and cold future' and are not a viable solution.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Hidden plots by secret schemes of powerful groups
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Cheap and abundant oil and natural gas have to go, Gore says – for poor people, anyway – in order for the planet to stop warming. This is the goal of the Inflation Reduction Act, a Biden regime initiative that Gore says is "primarily a climate act."
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text implies that there is a hidden plot to impose a 'green' agenda on the population, particularly targeting poor people, and that this is a goal of powerful groups or governments.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Climate cultists push bizarre scare language about weather: "rain bombs" and "boiling oceans"
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text criticizes the climate movement by using the term 'climate cultists' and implying that their language is 'bizarre' and scare-mongering, thus challenging the motives and integrity of the climate movement.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Gore would know about fossil fuel use, seeing as how he uses lots and lots of it – much more than the average person, in fact – to power his lavish, sprawling estates and fuel his private jets.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text criticizes Al Gore, a prominent figure in the climate movement and a former vice president, by highlighting the discrepancy between his words and actions, implying hypocrisy and challenging his integrity and competence as an authority on climate change.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: They have been losing their minds for decades. I have been hearing about these end of the world scenarios since I was a kid. And not one of their predictions has come true.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text downplays the significance of climate change by stating that past predictions have not come true, implying that the current warnings are also unlikely to be accurate, and thus minimizing the impact of climate change.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: A few years ago, someone (John Nolte?) listed all of their bigger predictions and their track record was 0-47
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text questions the scientific consensus on climate change by highlighting the alleged failure of past predictions, thus raising doubts about the accuracy and reliability of climate science.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: The legislation contains $369 billion, not including tax credits, in appropriation funds for "renewable energy," meaning windmills, solar panels, and a very dark and cold future for humanity.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text expresses skepticism about green technologies by implying that they will lead to a 'dark and cold future for humanity', thus criticizing the potential consequences of transitioning to renewable energy.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Cheap and abundant oil and natural gas have to go, Gore says – for poor people, anyway – in order for the planet to stop warming.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote criticizes Gore's climate policy by implying it unfairly targets poor people and ignores the benefits of cheap and abundant energy.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Criticism of institutions and authorities
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Gore would know about fossil fuel use, seeing as how he uses lots and lots of it – much more than the average person, in fact – to power his lavish, sprawling estates and fuel his private jets.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote challenges Gore's integrity and credibility on climate issues by highlighting his personal use of fossil fuels despite advocating against them.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: They have been losing their minds for decades. I have been hearing about these end of the world scenarios since I was a kid. And not one of their predictions has come true.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote downplays the significance of climate change by suggesting that past predictions have been incorrect and exaggerated.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Questioning the measurements and science
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: A few years ago, someone (John Nolte?) listed all of their bigger predictions and their track record was 0-47
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote raises doubts about the scientific consensus on climate change by pointing out the alleged failure of past predictions.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Climate cultists push bizarre scare language about weather: “rain bombs” and “boiling oceans”
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote criticizes the climate movement by implying that it uses exaggerated and misleading language to scare people.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Hidden plots by secret schemes of powerful groups
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Unless America and all other sovereign nations open up their borders to an illegal alien onslaught, the climate crisis will only continue to worsen, Gore went on to allege.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote suggests that Gore's climate agenda may be linked to a broader scheme to undermine national sovereignty and borders.
